page: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the t3.xlarge in the lower tiers of compute capability. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests register in the bottom 25%, reflecting its burstable CPU allocation model. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large blocks, similarly fall into the bottom 25% category. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as Passmark encryption and single-threaded gzip compression, also rank in the weak tier, while complex workloads like Geekbench ray tracing, image processing, and small-model LLM inference fall into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3.xlarge offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require continuous high CPU utilization. Because it utilizes a burstable CPU credit model, it is highly resource-dense and economical for baseline operations with occasional spikes. This makes it suitable for development environments, low-traffic web serving, code repositories, and small database operations that can tolerate variable compute performance.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, clocked at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. Benchmark results consistently place the instance in the lower performance tiers, with CPU, memory bandwidth, and compression tests scoring in the bottom 25% or bottom 10%. Consequently, the t3.xlarge is a cost-effective option for non-continuous workloads. It is best suited for development environments, code repositories, low-traffic web serving, and small database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for budget-conscious, variable workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for low-cost, variable workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly burstable instance with balanced memory and compute resources for development environments and low-traffic applications.

Burstable CPU allocation model utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage or GPU
Favorable qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with variable CPU demands
